en.extract:
The white-eyed starling (Aplonis brunneicapillus) is a species of starling in the family Sturnidae. It is found in the Solomon Islands archipelago. Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests and subtropical or tropical swamps. It is threatened by habitat loss.

The '''white-eyed starling''' ('''''Aplonis brunneicapillus''''') is a species of [[starling]] in the family [[Sturnidae]]. It is found in the [[Solomon Islands archipelago]]. Its natural [[habitat]]s are subtropical or tropical moist lowland [[forest]]s and subtropical or tropical [[swamp]]s. It is threatened by [[habitat loss]].
